column2: What two conditions must be true to install an OS from a USB drive?
column4: The USB drive must be bootable, and the computer must support booting from USB.
column5: A USB installer is local boot media. If either the media is not bootable or the system cannot boot from USB, the installation cannot start that way.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic Installation Boot Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician wants a computer to boot from the network and start an OS installation from a remote source. Which boot method fits?
column4: PXE network boot.
column5: PXE is the network-boot path for remote installation. The target computer must support PXE booting.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic Installation Network Source::Messer-v140

column2: A user needs Windows and Linux available on the same computer and wants to choose between them at startup. What installation approach supports this?
column4: Configure a multiboot system with separate bootable operating-system installations.
column5: Multiboot presents a boot menu for selecting among installed operating systems. It is different from replacing the old OS with a clean install.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic Installation Multiboot Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which installation type should be chosen when the goal is to start over with a fresh OS environment and the existing data does not need to remain in place?
column4: A clean install.
column5: A clean install wipes or replaces the existing environment. Backups and application reinstall plans matter before choosing it on a user system.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 3-4
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic HighYield Installation Backup Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which upgrade method keeps existing applications, documents, and settings when the upgrade path supports it?
column4: An in-place upgrade.
column5: An in-place upgrade starts from inside the existing OS. It is the preservation choice, but it still depends on supported upgrade paths and compatibility.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 4
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic HighYield Upgrade Installation Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which installation method deploys a prepared clone or standardized build to systems quickly?
column4: Image deployment.
column5: Image deployment is useful when many devices should receive the same baseline configuration. It is not the same as preserving each user's existing local applications and files.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic HighYield Deployment Installation Source::Messer-v140

column2: A company ships laptops directly to remote users and wants the installation and company-specific configuration to complete with minimal user input. Which deployment approach fits?
column4: Zero-touch deployment.
column5: Zero-touch deployment automates the installation and configuration workflow so a remote user can receive a system that sets itself up.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic HighYield Deployment Automation Source::Messer-v140

column2: What is the purpose of a recovery partition during OS installation or recovery?
column4: It is a hidden partition that stores installation or recovery files.
column5: A recovery partition must already exist to be useful. Do not assume it is available on every system or that it performs every kind of upgrade.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic Recovery Installation Source::Messer-v140

column2: What is a repair installation used for during Windows installation work?
column4: It is used to fix problems with the existing Windows OS without modifying user files.
column5: A repair installation targets the current Windows installation. It is different from a clean install, which starts over, and from an in-place upgrade, which moves an existing OS to a newer supported version.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic Repair Windows Source::Messer-v140

column2: During an OS install, the installer cannot see the target storage controller. What should the technician consider loading?
column4: The appropriate third-party storage or disk-controller driver.
column5: Some installers need additional drivers before they can access the storage hardware. This is different from choosing a different installation type.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic HighYield Drivers Installation Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which partition style is the better fit for a modern UEFI system that needs many partitions or very large partitions?
column4: GPT.
column5: GPT is the newer partition style and removes the old MBR limits around primary partition count and 2 TB partition size.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 3-4
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic HighYield Partitioning UEFI Source::Messer-v140

column2: What are the major limitations of MBR partitioning compared with GPT?
column4: MBR is limited to 2 TB partitions and up to four primary partitions per hard disk.
column5: One primary partition can be marked active. Extra logical partitions require an extended partition, but that does not remove MBR's older design limits.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 3-4
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic HighYield Partitioning Source::Messer-v140

column2: How does a full format differ from a quick format during installation?
column4: A quick format creates a new file table, while a full format writes zeros across the disk and checks for bad sectors.
column5: Quick format can make data appear erased without overwriting all of it. Full format is slower and destructive, so the choice matters when data recovery or disk checking is a concern.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 4
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic Format Storage Source::Messer-v140

column2: Before repartitioning or performing a clean install on a user's drive, what data-protection step should be completed?
column4: Back up existing data and user preferences that may be needed later.
column5: Partitioning, formatting, and clean installs can cause serious data loss. Protect user data before making destructive storage changes.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 4
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic HighYield Backup Upgrade Source::Messer-v140

column2: What compatibility checks should be performed before relying on an OS upgrade?
column4: Check hardware requirements and verify application and driver compatibility.
column5: Compatibility checks reduce the risk of a failed upgrade or a working OS with broken devices and applications.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 4
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic HighYield Compatibility Upgrade Source::Messer-v140

column2: Why should product lifecycle be considered before choosing a Windows upgrade target?
column4: Support windows and update availability affect whether the OS version will continue receiving security fixes, bug fixes, and feature updates.
column5: Do not choose an upgrade target only because it installs successfully. The target should remain supportable after deployment.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 4
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic Lifecycle Updates Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician has OS installation files stored on a removable SSD or external drive that can boot through USB. Which boot-source category fits?
column4: Solid-state/flash drive or external/hot-swappable drive boot media.
column5: The exam lists these as separate boot-method examples, but both are local storage-based installation sources. The device still has to be bootable and supported by the target computer.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic Installation Boot Source::Messer-v140

column2: A technician starts an OS installation that downloads the installer from an online source instead of using local USB media or a local network server. Which boot method category fits?
column4: Internet-based installation.
column5: Internet-based installation uses an online source for the installation workflow. Messer lists examples such as Linux distribution installers, macOS Recovery installation, and Windows updates.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic Installation Boot Source::Messer-v140

column2: Which installation type uses a local server, shared drive, or Internet source to install the operating system across the network?
column4: Remote network installation.
column5: PXE describes booting from the network. Remote network installation describes where the installation source is located and how the OS installation is delivered.
column9: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column10: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Basic Installation Network Source::Messer-v140
